meaning: adv.笨拙地,粗陋地,不漂亮地
examples: He'd move his fingers clumsily on the piano, and then she'd take his place.
He was clumsily trying to grab at Alfred's arms.
Her numb leg gave way beneath her and she stumbled clumsily.
Wine geeks to a man or woman hate super-rich, clumsily tannic, high-alcohol wines--just ask them.
Anyway, governments in rich countries also intervene a lot, and very often as clumsily.
